description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a robust metal framework. The panels are elevated above the ground by a series of angled metal supports, forming a canopy-like structure. Beneath the panels, there is a platform with a checkered pattern, and adjacent to it, a sloped, boxy enclosure that likely houses electrical or mechanical components. Two square, metallic grates or covers are positioned nearby, possibly for access to underground utilities or ventilation. The overall design is industrial and utilitarian, with a focus on maximizing solar exposure while providing structural stability. The materials appear to be primarily metal and glass, with a sleek, modern finish.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a metal frame with a checkered platform and adjacent utility enclosure, accompanied by two metallic grates.
description_view_0: The solar panel array is seen from an angle highlighting the checkered platform and the sloped utility enclosure on the right.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the length of the metal frame and the alignment of the solar panels, with the utility enclosure partially visible.
description_view_2: The sloped enclosure is prominent in the foreground, with the solar panels and two metallic grates clearly separated in the background.
description_view_3: The metal frame and support structure are most visible, with the two metallic grates positioned apart from the main installation.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum supports and a checkered metal platform
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8 panels
